inventory_sections.reading: Concept stock mapping must separate business truth from looser market-facing associations.
Concept mappings need explicit source-quality tags so rumor-like mappings never get treated like confirmed ones.
A concept mapping only becomes research-relevant when the market actually confirms it as a live board or theme trade.
Concept membership must be treated as point-in-time and bounded, not as a permanent static tag.

interpretation: V1.3 should begin from concept-mapping infrastructure because concept stocks in A-share often trade on market-recognized theme overlays rather than on static industry labels.
That means the first bounded inventory must freeze mapping modes, source quality, point-in-time bounds, and market-confirmation requirements before any larger registry build starts.
The next legal action after this inventory is a bounded concept-seed, not direct ingestion or direct model work.
